closed_reason: Turn 2 Codex peer-verify reproduced C1-C5 directly. The settings UI slice was valid overall, but channel_id validation initially accepted non-hex 32-char strings; Turn 2 fixed that defect in place and sealed the session.

contract_checkpoints.C1: PASS. sister main@d97514e, live d97514e, remote d97514e, merge-base OK.
contract_checkpoints.C2: PASS after fix. Dynamic row add/delete and streamer field editing work. channel_id validation now checks real 32-hex form.
contract_checkpoints.C3: PASS. Legacy single-streamer config loads as one row and remains backward-compatible through legacy mirror fields.
contract_checkpoints.C4: PASS. Saved cfg['streamers'] round-trips through normalize_streamers() to the same canonical list.
contract_checkpoints.C5: PASS. Live Tk UI verification, settings_ui import smoke, output rebuild regression check, and both validators passed.

decisions: Turn 1: Streamer scalar inputs were moved out of FIELDS into a dedicated multi-streamer section.
Turn 1: Saved config keeps a canonical streamers list and mirrors the first streamer into legacy scalar fields for downstream compatibility.
Turn 1: Empty streamer rows are blocked at save time so normalize_streamers() fallback is not silently triggered by a blank UI.
Turn 2: channel_id validation was tightened from length-only to real 32-hex checking.
meta_improvements: Legacy scalar mirrors preserve tray/build/runtime fallback compatibility while the UI moves to canonical multi-streamer editing.
Real 32-hex validation closes a practical misconfiguration gap that the first implementation still allowed.
